description: Application logs are plain text or inconsistent. Production needs JSON logging (e.g., pino, winston, or loguru) for observability.
Errors are logged to console but not captured by a service like Sentry or GlitchTip.
Load balancers and orchestrators need a dedicated health check endpoint to monitor service status.
The process kills active connections/requests on SIGTERM instead of waiting for them to finish.
Using db-push or manual schema changes instead of versioned migrations (e.g., drizzle-kit migrate).
Foreign keys or common query columns lack indexes, causing sequential scans.
Public API routes are vulnerable to brute force or DoS because there is no per-IP/user limit.
Routes lack strict schema validation (e.g., Zod, Pydantic) and may be vulnerable to injection or malformed data.
Email sending or heavy processing happens on the request path instead of being offloaded to a queue.
The API contract is not documented in a machine-readable format.
